data_shapes.Board: Array<Cell> of length 9, indexed 0..8 in row-major order (0,1,2 = top row; 6,7,8 = bottom row)

GameState.moveCount: number (0 at start, increments per applied move)

Outcome.line: Array<number> of 3 cell indices when status='win'; otherwise null

createBoard.description: Returns a fresh GameState with all cells null, currentPlayer='X', moveCount=0.

applyMove.signature: (state: GameState, index: number, player: Player) -> GameState
applyMove.description: Returns a new GameState with the given cell filled. Throws (or returns sentinel — Builder picks one and documents) if index out of [0..8], cell is non-null, or player != state.currentPlayer. Does not mutate input.
applyMove.error_contract: Builder must document whether illegal moves throw or return a sentinel; controller-and-shell must adapt to that choice.

checkOutcome.description: Inspects the board for any of the 8 winning lines. Returns winner and line indices on win, 'draw' if board is full with no winner, else 'in_progress'.

legalMoves.description: Returns indices of empty cells in ascending order. Empty array when board is full.

guarantees: All methods are pure: no I/O, no module-global mutable state, no DOM access.
Return values are new objects; inputs are never mutated.
Indexing convention is row-major 0..8 and is stable across the whole project.

notes: ai-opponent calls legalMoves to enumerate options, applyMove (on a cloned state) to test hypothetical outcomes, and checkOutcome to detect win/block opportunities. It must NOT reimplement the 8-line win check.
